title: Extract followup and corrective logic into scheduler/followups.py
description: Move followup creation, shared followup scope population, scope syncing, and corrective-bead logic into `scheduler/followups.py` without changing followup semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Followup and corrective helpers live in `src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py`.
Shared followup scope population and sync behavior remain unchanged.
Corrective creation and escalation behavior are preserved exactly.

handoff_summary.completed: Created `src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py` with `FollowupManager` class containing all corrective helpers (`_is_corrective_bead`, `_already_retried_after_corrective`, `_corrective_children`, `_can_plan_corrective`, `_create_corrective_bead`, `_escalate_blocked_bead`, `_requeue_parent_after_corrective_completion`) and followup helpers (`_create_followups`, `_merge_unique_items`, `_merge_conflict_risks`, `_sync_followup_scope`, `_sync_followup_dependencies`, `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files`, and all the planner-owned/legacy followup lookup helpers). Updated `execution.py` to instantiate `FollowupManager` as `self._followups` and remove the now-moved methods. Updated `finalize.py` and `core.py` to call `self._executor._followups.*` instead of `self._executor.*`. Removed unused `BEAD_READY` import and dead `followup_suffixes`/`corrective_suffix`/etc. instance variables from `BeadExecutor`. Both `from src.agent_takt.scheduler import Scheduler` and `from src.agent_takt.scheduler.followups import FollowupManager` import cleanly.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — all acceptance criteria are satisfied.
handoff_summary.risks: None. The extraction is purely mechanical — logic is identical, only moved to a new class. The delegation chain (`core.py` → `_executor._followups.*`, `finalize.py` → `_executor._followups.*`, `execution.py` → `_followups.*`) preserves all call semantics.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester bead should run the full test suite to verify no regressions in followup creation, corrective bead lifecycle, and scope population paths.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. Files touched are all within the scheduler package and were already claimed by this feature tree.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose to create a `FollowupManager` class (rather than module-level functions) for consistency with the existing `BeadFinalizer` and `BeadExecutor` patterns, and to keep `storage` and `config` bound once at construction rather than threaded through every call. All callers go through `_executor._followups.*` rather than being given a direct reference to `FollowupManager`, keeping the access chain shallow and consistent with the existing two-level `_executor._finalizer` pattern. `FOLLOWUP_AGENT_TYPES` constant moved from `execution.py` to `followups.py` since all its consumers moved there.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The tester should verify: (1) followup beads (test/docs/review) are still created after developer bead completion; (2) planner-owned shared followup reuse still works; (3) corrective bead creation and escalation trigger correctly on blocked beads; (4) `_populate_shared_followup_touched_files` still aggregates touched_files from done dependencies; (5) `_requeue_parent_after_corrective_completion` still re-queues blocked tester/review parents. The full test suite in `tests/` should cover all of these paths via the existing scheduler integration tests.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — the extraction is complete as specified. The `_executor._followups.*` delegation chain is one level deeper than the previous `_executor.*` direct calls; a future bead could optionally expose `FollowupManager` directly on `Scheduler` to flatten that, but it is not required by this spec.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. Files touched are all within the scheduler package and were already claimed by this feature tree.

execution_history.summary: Extracted all followup and corrective bead logic from `execution.py` into `scheduler/followups.py` as a `FollowupManager` class. Updated `execution.py`, `finalize.py`, and `core.py` to delegate through `_executor._followups.*`. No behavioral changes — pure mechanical extraction with import cleanup.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
